channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: France Records 1,000 Excess Deaths During Heatwave, Elderly Hit Hard

media:content.media:description: The sharpest increases involved people dying at home

item.description: Public Health France said areas under red alert for heat had been particularly badly affected, and 85 percent of the deaths had been those aged 65 and over.
item.content:encoded: Public Health France said areas under red alert for heat had been particularly badly affected, and 85 percent of the deaths had been those aged 65 and over.

item.title: "I Just Assumed": Zohran Mamdani Defends Swimming Pool Dress Code Violation

media:content.media:description: This was not the first time Mamdani got into the water wearing a business suit.

item.description: Zohran Mamdani jumped into the pool with Manhattan Borough President Brad Hoylman-Sigal and Parks Commissioner Tricia Shimamura to mark the opening of the city's outdoor pool season.
item.content:encoded: Zohran Mamdani jumped into the pool with Manhattan Borough President Brad Hoylman-Sigal and Parks Commissioner Tricia Shimamura to mark the opening of the city's outdoor pool season.

item.title: Trump Warns Iran "Won't Exist" After Strikes, Gets "Experience Hell" Reply

media:content.media:description: The US Central Command (CENTCOM) also released a 38-second video of the strikes on Iran on X.

item.description: The US had struck Iranian missile and drone storage sites and coastal radar positions on Friday, too, after it accused Tehran of attacking a cargo ship that was transiting the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1119371/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>The US had struck Iranian missile and drone storage sites and coastal radar positions on Friday, too, after it accused Tehran of attacking a cargo ship that was transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Australian Man Arrested In Thailand After Teenager's Body Found In Suitcase

media:content.media:description: Media reports said the 46-year-old suspect was apprehended on Friday

item.description: Investigators were awaiting the results of a post-mortem examination and "other evidence" in the case before filing charges
item.content:encoded: Investigators were awaiting the results of a post-mortem examination and "other evidence" in the case before filing charges

item.title: Heatwave Sweeps Europe, Breaks Temperature Records Across Countries

media:content.media:description: German authorities issued extreme heat warnings nearly nationwide on Saturday

item.description: Scientists said the stifling heatwave would have been virtually impossible without man-made climate change
item.content:encoded: Scientists said the stifling heatwave would have been virtually impossible without man-made climate change

item.title: Indian Student Found Dead Hours After Attending Birthday Party In London

media:content.media:description: Reddy was pursuing a master's degree at De Montfort University (file)

item.description: While his roommate claimed Srinath Reddy had hung himself, the official cause of his death in London is not known yet.
item.content:encoded: While his roommate claimed Srinath Reddy had hung himself, the official cause of his death in London is not known yet.

item.title: An Indian Explosives Company Faces Questions From A War Half A World Away

media:content.media:description: Illustration by Prapti Upadhayay.

item.description: An explosives manufacturer from India suddenly found itself named alongside Sudanese military-linked companies, Egyptian suppliers and international recruitment networks accused of prolonging a civil war that has devastated an entire nation.
item.content:encoded: An explosives manufacturer from India suddenly found itself named alongside Sudanese military-linked companies, Egyptian suppliers and international recruitment networks accused of prolonging a civil war that has devastated an entire nation.

item.title: Long, Painful Wait By A Pile Of Rubble In Earthquake-Hit Venezuela

media:content.media:description: The earthquakes killed more than 1,400 people and left some 50,000 missing

item.description: As the crews worked, Friday, it had been two days since that display of nature's power. One more day, and it is generally considered that a search for survivors becomes a search for bodies.
item.content:encoded: As the crews worked, Friday, it had been two days since that display of nature's power. One more day, and it is generally considered that a search for survivors becomes a search for bodies.

item.title: Chinese Dissident Who Fled To South Korea On Rubber Boat Reaches Canada

media:content.media:description: After several failed attempts to flee China, he initially made it to South Korea

item.description: Dong Guangping, a 68-year-old former policeman, has been a thorn in Beijing's side for advocating political reform and human rights, and served multiple prison stints over the years.
item.content:encoded: Dong Guangping, a 68-year-old former policeman, has been a thorn in Beijing's side for advocating political reform and human rights, and served multiple prison stints over the years.

item.title: Venezuela Earthquakes Live: Another Earthquake Of Magnitude 5.6 Hits Venezuela

media:content.media:description: Earthquake in Venezuela Updates

item.description: Venezuela earthquake LIVE Updates: The second earthquake struck just a minute later, with a 7.5-magnitude, and its epicentre was 16 kilometres southwest of Moron.
item.content:encoded: Venezuela earthquake LIVE Updates: The second earthquake struck just a minute later, with a 7.5-magnitude, and its epicentre was 16 kilometres southwest of Moron.

item.title: Trump Taps Former Trooper Lance Schroyer To Lead Immigration Agency

media:content.media:description: Schroyer's nomination comes after former ICE director Todd Lyons resigned at the end of May

item.description: Trump said on his Truth Social platform that his new pick for the immigration enforcement agency is a former US Marine and a "PATRIOT with real operational experience.
item.content:encoded: Trump said on his Truth Social platform that his new pick for the immigration enforcement agency is a former US Marine and a "PATRIOT with real operational experience.

item.title: Hezbollah Slams Israel-Lebanon Deal, Questions Its Effectiveness

media:content.media:description: Lebanese state news agency reported an Israeli drone strike near the southern city of Nabatiyeh

item.description: The agreement signed Friday in Washington links Israel's withdrawal from Lebanon to Hezbollah's disarmament, something the group rejects.
item.content:encoded: The agreement signed Friday in Washington links Israel's withdrawal from Lebanon to Hezbollah's disarmament, something the group rejects.

item.title: US-Iran War Live Updates: US-Iran Exchange Strikes, Deal Under Pressure

media:content.media:description: US-Iran War Live Updates: US, Iran signed a 14-point deal to end the war on June 17

item.description: US-Iran War Live Updates: The clashes brought new tension to the negotiating process meant to end a war launched by the United States and Israel at the end of February.
item.content:encoded: US-Iran War Live Updates: The clashes brought new tension to the negotiating process meant to end a war launched by the United States and Israel at the end of February.

item.title: 6 Terrorists Killed After Attack On Pak Army Base In Karachi, 4 Soldiers Dead

media:content.media:description: Special units such as the Rapid Response Force and police commandos were dispatched to the scene

item.description: Pakistan has witnessed a surge in extremist attacks targeting police and security forces in recent years.
item.content:encoded: Pakistan has witnessed a surge in extremist attacks targeting police and security forces in recent years.

item.title: Challenges That Lie Ahead For The US-Israel-Lebanon Deal

media:content.media:description: Netanyahu said displaced Lebanese civilians will not be allowed to return home to occupied areas.

item.description: Lebanon and Israel, under US sponsorship, signed an agreement on Friday hoping to end hostilities between them, but experts say it does not guarantee Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon.
item.content:encoded: Lebanon and Israel, under US sponsorship, signed an agreement on Friday hoping to end hostilities between them, but experts say it does not guarantee Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon.

item.title: Venezuela Earthquakes May Have Affected Nearly 7 Million People: UN

media:content.media:description: The national death count stands at 920, while people remain trapped.

item.description: Entire buildings have crumbled in La Guaira, located to the north of Caracas, following devastating twin tremors of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 on Wednesday.
item.content:encoded: Entire buildings have crumbled in La Guaira, located to the north of Caracas, following devastating twin tremors of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 on Wednesday.

item.title: Many Similarities In Leadership Style: US Envoy Says PM Modi, Trump Think Alike

media:content.media:description: Sergio Gor said the president holds both PM Modi and India in high regard

item.description: US Ambassador to India, Sergio Gor dismissed the idea that there are strains in bilateral relations and said that the close personal relationship between both leaders is the cornerstone of India-US ties.
item.content:encoded: US Ambassador to India, Sergio Gor dismissed the idea that there are strains in bilateral relations and said that the close personal relationship between both leaders is the cornerstone of India-US ties.

item.title: Schools In US To Introduce AI-Powered Humanoid Robots As Teaching Partners

media:content.media:description: Representative Image

item.description: While school officials have described the initiative as a step toward the future of learning.
item.content:encoded: While school officials have described the initiative as a step toward the future of learning.

item.title: 10,000 Vespas Swarm Rome's Historic Sites To Mark Scooter's 80th Anniversary

media:content.media:description: Piaggio has sold about 20 million Vespas worldwide since 1946

item.description: Vespa-borne visitors converged on the Eternal City's cobblestone streets to celebrate a brand they likewise view as timeless. If for only a day, Ferrari and Ducati were forgotten as the little Vespa left them in its dust.
item.content:encoded: Vespa-borne visitors converged on the Eternal City's cobblestone streets to celebrate a brand they likewise view as timeless. If for only a day, Ferrari and Ducati were forgotten as the little Vespa left them in its dust.

item.title: MacBook, iPad Price Hikes Only The Beginning: Here's What You May Pay Next

media:content.media:description: Apple's steep price hikes in Macs and iPads are spotlighting the chip crunch.

item.description: An unprecedented AI data centre boom is triggering a massive $670 billion surge in the global memory market, expanding it to $890 billion by 2026, according to Morgan Stanley.
item.content:encoded: An unprecedented AI data centre boom is triggering a massive $670 billion surge in the global memory market, expanding it to $890 billion by 2026, according to Morgan Stanley.
